message.content: why does my build-autonomous skill not show up here?

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Two reasons, and the first is the decisive one:

**1. The skills aren't installed.** `~/.claude/skills/` doesn't exist:
```
ls: cannot access '/home/gherlein/.claude/skills': No such file or directory
```
Claude Code reads skills from `~/.claude/skills/`, **not** from this repo. As your own `CLAUDE.md` says, this repo is a *source template* — nothing here is active until you run `./safe-install`. That's why **none** of your 38 custom skills (`brainstorming`, `build-autonomous`, `orchestrate`, etc.) appear in the available-skills list — only the built-in/plugin skills (`deep-research`, `code-review`, `verify`, …) do.

**2. Even once installed, `build-autonomous` is opted out of the model's list.** Its frontmatter has:
```yaml
disable-model-invocation: true
```
That flag tells the harness *I* shouldn't auto-list or auto-invoke it. It would still be reachable when **you** type `/build-autonomous` directly, but it won't appear in the model-facing skills catalog you saw above.

So: install with `./safe-install` to make it (and the others) usable at all, and it'll work as a user-typed `/build-autonomous` — by design it stays out of the auto-invoke list.

Want me to run `./safe-install`, or check whether your active Claude Code is even pointed at `~/.claude`?

lastPrompt: why does my build-autonomous skill not show up here?

82	if [[ -f "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" ]]; then
83	    NEEDS_BACKUP=true
84	fi
85	if [[ -d "$SKILLS_DIR" ]] && find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d &>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
86	    NEEDS_BACKUP=true
87	fi

90	        NEEDS_BACKUP=true
91	    fi
92	    if [[ -d "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" ]]; then
93	        NEEDS_BACKUP=true
94	    fi
95	fi
96	
97	# Back up existing user config files (not operational data)
98	if [[ "$NEEDS_BACKUP" == "true" ]]; then
99	    echo "Backing up existing configuration to $BACKUP_DIR/"

101	    if [[ -f "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" ]]; then
102	        cp "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" "$BACKUP_DIR/CLAUDE.md"

105	    if [[ -d "$SKILLS_DIR" ]] && find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d &>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
106	        mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR/skills"
107	        cp -r "$SKILLS_DIR/"*/ "$BACKUP_DIR/skills/"
108	        echo "  backed up skills/"
109	    fi
110	    if [[ "$TARGET_DIR" == "$GLOBAL_DIR" ]]; then
111	        if [[ -f "$GLOBAL_INDEX" ]]; then
112	            cp "$GLOBAL_INDEX" "$BACKUP_DIR/INDEX.md"
113	            echo "  backed up INDEX.md"
114	        fi
115	        if [[ -d "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" ]]; then
116	            cp -r "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" "$BACKUP_DIR/security-rules"
117	            echo "  backed up security-rules/"
118	        fi
119	    fi
120	fi
121	
122	# Install CLAUDE.md
123	echo "Installing CLAUDE.md -> $TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"
124	mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD")"
125	cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/CLAUDE.md" "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"
126	
127	# Install skills
128	echo "Installing skills -> $SKILLS_DIR/"
129	mkdir -p "$SKILLS_DIR"
130	
131	# Remove old flat .md files (legacy structure)
132	if ls "$SKILLS_DIR/"*.md &>/dev/null; then
133	    echo "  removing legacy flat .md skill files"
134	    rm -f "$SKILLS_DIR/"*.md
135	fi
136	
137	# Remove only skill directories that this repo ships, preserving user-created skills
138	for skill_dir in "$SCRIPT_DIR/skills/"*; do
139	    if [[ ! -d "$skill_dir" ]]; then continue; fi
140	    skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")
141	    target="$SKILLS_DIR/$skill_name"
142	    if [[ -d "$target" ]]; then
143	        rm -rf "$target"
144	    fi
145	done
146	
147	# Copy all skill directories (one at a time for compatibility)
148	COPIED=0
149	for skill_dir in "$SCRIPT_DIR/skills/"*; do
150	    if [[ ! -d "$skill_dir" ]]; then
151	        continue
152	    fi
153	    skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")
154	    echo "  copying $skill_name"
155	    cp -r "$skill_dir" "$SKILLS_DIR/" || echo "  ERROR: failed to copy $skill_name"
156	    COPIED=$((COPIED + 1))
157	done
158	
159	SKILL_COUNT=$(find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
160	echo "  installed $SKILL_COUNT skills (attempted $COPIED)"
161	
162	# Install INDEX.md (always global, even for project installs)
163	echo "Installing INDEX.md -> $GLOBAL_INDEX"
164	mkdir -p "$GLOBAL_DIR"
165	cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/INDEX.md" "$GLOBAL_INDEX"
166	
167	# Install security-rules (always global, even for project installs)
168	echo "Installing security-rules -> $GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES/"
169	rm -rf "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES"
170	cp -r "$SCRIPT_DIR/security-rules" "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES"
171	RULE_COUNT=$(find "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" -name "*.md" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
172	echo "  installed $RULE_COUNT security rule files"
173	
174	# Summary
175	echo ""
176	echo "Installation complete."
177	echo ""
178	echo "Installed:"
179	echo "  $TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"
180	echo "  $SKILLS_DIR/ ($SKILL_COUNT skills)"
181	echo "  $GLOBAL_INDEX"
182	echo "  $GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES/ ($RULE_COUNT rule files)"
183	echo ""
184	if [[ "$NEEDS_BACKUP" == "true" ]]; then
185	    echo "Previous config backed up to: $BACKUP_DIR/"
186	fi
187	echo ""
188	echo "Operational files were NOT modified:"
189	echo "  settings.json, .credentials.json, history.jsonl, projects/, etc."

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# safe-install: Install Claude Code user configuration from this repo
#
# Usage:
#   ./safe-install                   Install globally to ~/.claude/
#   ./safe-install /path/to/project  Install to a project directory
#
# Global install:
#   1. Backs up any existing CLAUDE.md, skills/, INDEX.md, security-rules/ in ~/.claude
#   2. Copies CLAUDE.md to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
#   3. Replaces only repo-shipped skills in ~/.claude/skills/
#   4. Preserves any user-created skill directories not shipped by this repo
#   5. Copies INDEX.md to ~/.claude/INDEX.md
#   6. Replaces ~/.claude/security-rules/ with repo-shipped rules
#
# Project install:
#   1. Backs up any existing CLAUDE.md and .claude/skills/ in the project
#   2. Copies CLAUDE.md to <project>/CLAUDE.md
#   3. Replaces only repo-shipped skills in <project>/.claude/skills/
#   4. Preserves any user-created skill directories not shipped by this repo
#   Note: INDEX.md and security-rules/ are always installed globally to ~/.claude/
#
# What it does NOT touch (Claude Code operational data):
#   .credentials.json, history.jsonl, settings.json, stats-cache.json,
#   cache/, debug/, file-history/, paste-cache/, plans/, plugins/,
#   projects/, session-env/, shell-snapshots/, tasks/, telemetry/, todos/

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

if [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; then
    if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: project directory does not exist: $1"
        exit 1
    fi
    PROJECT_DIR="$(cd "$1" && pwd)"
    TARGET_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/.claude"
    TARGET_CLAUDE_MD="$PROJECT_DIR/CLAUDE.md"
    SKILLS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/skills"
    echo "Installing to project: $PROJECT_DIR"
else
    TARGET_DIR="$HOME/.claude"
    TARGET_CLAUDE_MD="$TARGET_DIR/CLAUDE.md"
    SKILLS_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/skills"
    echo "Installing globally to: $TARGET_DIR"
fi

BACKUP_DIR="$TARGET_DIR/backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"

# Sanity checks
if [[ ! -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/CLAUDE.md" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: CLAUDE.md not found in $SCRIPT_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/skills" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: skills/ not found in $SCRIPT_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/INDEX.md" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: INDEX.md not found in $SCRIPT_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -d "$SCRIPT_DIR/security-rules" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: security-rules/ not found in $SCRIPT_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure target directory exists
mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR"

# Global-only paths (INDEX.md and security-rules are always global, not project-level)
GLOBAL_DIR="$HOME/.claude"
GLOBAL_INDEX="$GLOBAL_DIR/INDEX.md"
GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES="$GLOBAL_DIR/security-rules"

# Determine what needs backing up
NEEDS_BACKUP=false
if [[ -f "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" ]]; then
    NEEDS_BACKUP=true
fi
if [[ -d "$SKILLS_DIR" ]] && find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d &>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
    NEEDS_BACKUP=true
fi
if [[ "$TARGET_DIR" == "$GLOBAL_DIR" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$GLOBAL_INDEX" ]]; then
        NEEDS_BACKUP=true
    fi
    if [[ -d "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" ]]; then
        NEEDS_BACKUP=true
    fi
fi

# Back up existing user config files (not operational data)
if [[ "$NEEDS_BACKUP" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "Backing up existing configuration to $BACKUP_DIR/"
    mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
    if [[ -f "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" ]]; then
        cp "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD" "$BACKUP_DIR/CLAUDE.md"
        echo "  backed up CLAUDE.md"
    fi
    if [[ -d "$SKILLS_DIR" ]] && find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d &>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
        mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR/skills"
        cp -r "$SKILLS_DIR/"*/ "$BACKUP_DIR/skills/"
        echo "  backed up skills/"
    fi
    if [[ "$TARGET_DIR" == "$GLOBAL_DIR" ]]; then
        if [[ -f "$GLOBAL_INDEX" ]]; then
            cp "$GLOBAL_INDEX" "$BACKUP_DIR/INDEX.md"
            echo "  backed up INDEX.md"
        fi
        if [[ -d "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" ]]; then
            cp -r "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" "$BACKUP_DIR/security-rules"
            echo "  backed up security-rules/"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Install CLAUDE.md
echo "Installing CLAUDE.md -> $TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD")"
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/CLAUDE.md" "$TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"

# Install skills
echo "Installing skills -> $SKILLS_DIR/"
mkdir -p "$SKILLS_DIR"

# Remove old flat .md files (legacy structure)
if ls "$SKILLS_DIR/"*.md &>/dev/null; then
    echo "  removing legacy flat .md skill files"
    rm -f "$SKILLS_DIR/"*.md
fi

# Remove only skill directories that this repo ships, preserving user-created skills
for skill_dir in "$SCRIPT_DIR/skills/"*; do
    if [[ ! -d "$skill_dir" ]]; then continue; fi
    skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")
    target="$SKILLS_DIR/$skill_name"
    if [[ -d "$target" ]]; then
        rm -rf "$target"
    fi
done

# Copy all skill directories (one at a time for compatibility)
COPIED=0
for skill_dir in "$SCRIPT_DIR/skills/"*; do
    if [[ ! -d "$skill_dir" ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    skill_name=$(basename "$skill_dir")
    echo "  copying $skill_name"
    cp -r "$skill_dir" "$SKILLS_DIR/" || echo "  ERROR: failed to copy $skill_name"
    COPIED=$((COPIED + 1))
done

SKILL_COUNT=$(find "$SKILLS_DIR" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "  installed $SKILL_COUNT skills (attempted $COPIED)"

# Install INDEX.md (always global, even for project installs)
echo "Installing INDEX.md -> $GLOBAL_INDEX"
mkdir -p "$GLOBAL_DIR"
cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/INDEX.md" "$GLOBAL_INDEX"

# Install security-rules (always global, even for project installs)
echo "Installing security-rules -> $GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES/"
rm -rf "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES"
cp -r "$SCRIPT_DIR/security-rules" "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES"
RULE_COUNT=$(find "$GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES" -name "*.md" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "  installed $RULE_COUNT security rule files"

# Summary
echo ""
echo "Installation complete."
echo ""
echo "Installed:"
echo "  $TARGET_CLAUDE_MD"
echo "  $SKILLS_DIR/ ($SKILL_COUNT skills)"
echo "  $GLOBAL_INDEX"
echo "  $GLOBAL_SECURITY_RULES/ ($RULE_COUNT rule files)"
echo ""
if [[ "$NEEDS_BACKUP" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "Previous config backed up to: $BACKUP_DIR/"
fi
echo ""
echo "Operational files were NOT modified:"
echo "  settings.json, .credentials.json, history.jsonl, projects/, etc."

content.text: The script is safe — it backs up existing config and only touches `CLAUDE.md`, `skills/`, `INDEX.md`, and `security-rules/`, leaving `settings.json`, credentials, and operational data alone. Since `~/.claude/skills/` doesn't exist yet, this is a clean first install. Running it:

content.text:
Done. All 39 skills installed to `~/.claude/skills/` (plus `INDEX.md` and 83 security-rule files). No operational data touched.

Two notes:
- **Restart your session to pick them up.** Skills are loaded at startup, so they won't appear in *this* session — open a fresh Claude Code session and they'll be there.
- **`build-autonomous` will only be user-invocable.** Because of its `disable-model-invocation: true` flag, it won't show in the auto-invoke catalog — but typing `/build-autonomous` will work.

content: Goal was to make your custom skills usable. They weren't installed, so I ran ./safe-install, which copied all 39 skills (including build-autonomous) to ~/.claude. Next: restart your Claude Code session to load them. (disable recaps in /config)
